title: The Weight of Goodbye
content: People often imagine that goodbyes happen in dramatic moments, filled with tears, desperate embraces, and words that echo forever. Ours was nothing like that. It happened on an ordinary Tuesday morning at a crowded train station where strangers hurried past us without noticing that our world was quietly changing. You smiled, but it was the kind of smile people wear when they are trying not to break. We promised to stay in touch, to visit whenever possible, and to believe that distance could never change what we felt. Months turned into years, and our conversations slowly became shorter, then less frequent, until silence settled between us like an unexpected guest. Sometimes I still return to that station, not because I expect to see you again, but because part of me never truly left. I have learned that the weight of goodbye is rarely carried in the moment itself. It grows quietly afterward, inside ordinary days, hidden in familiar places, and in memories that refuse to disappear.
